verses.text: 'The heavens declare the glory of God,' day after day and night after night pouring out wordless speech that reaches the ends of the earth. Creation is God's first sermon. The sky preaches His greatness to every land without a syllable.

'The heavens declare the glory of God,' day after day and night after night pouring out wordless speech that reaches the ends of the earth. Creation is God's first sermon. The sky preaches His greatness to every land without a syllable.

'The heavens declare the glory of God,' day after day and night after night pouring out wordless speech that reaches the ends of the earth. Creation is God's first sermon. The sky preaches His greatness to every land without a syllable.

'The heavens declare the glory of God,' day after day and night after night pouring out wordless speech that reaches the ends of the earth. Creation is God's first sermon. The sky preaches His greatness to every land without a syllable.

The sun, like a bridegroom and a strong runner, crosses the heavens, and nothing is hidden from its heat. Its daily circuit displays God's order. The faithful round of the sun testifies to the constancy of its Maker.

The sun, like a bridegroom and a strong runner, crosses the heavens, and nothing is hidden from its heat. Its daily circuit displays God's order. The faithful round of the sun testifies to the constancy of its Maker.

The law of the LORD is perfect, converting the soul; His testimony, statutes, and commandments are sure, right, and pure, enlightening the eyes. Scripture is God's second and clearer book. His word does what the heavens cannot: it revives and transforms the soul.

The law of the LORD is perfect, converting the soul; His testimony, statutes, and commandments are sure, right, and pure, enlightening the eyes. Scripture is God's second and clearer book. His word does what the heavens cannot: it revives and transforms the soul.

The law of the LORD is perfect, converting the soul; His testimony, statutes, and commandments are sure, right, and pure, enlightening the eyes. Scripture is God's second and clearer book. His word does what the heavens cannot: it revives and transforms the soul.

These words are more precious than fine gold and sweeter than honey, and in keeping them is great reward. God's word is both treasure and delight. Those who heed it find both warning and rich blessing.

These words are more precious than fine gold and sweeter than honey, and in keeping them is great reward. God's word is both treasure and delight. Those who heed it find both warning and rich blessing.

David asks to be cleansed from secret faults and kept from presumptuous sins, praying that his words and thoughts be 'acceptable in thy sight.' The word that searches him drives him to prayer. To behold God's law is to long to be made wholly pleasing to Him.
